experience.title: Undergraduate Researcher
experience.description:
Researched fuel cost minimization for gunbarrel natural gas pipeline networks by optimizing compressor operations; the proposed algorithm significantly reduces computation time (>95%) without compromising the optimality in most cases.
•	Publication: A Dynamic Programming Approach to Power Consumption Minimization in Gunbarrel Natural Gas Networks with Non-identical Compressor Unites, INFORMS Journal on Computing, 2018, accepted

projects.name: Machine Learning: Search Conversion Rate Prediction
projects.description:
Built classification models to predict Search Conversion Rate based on 5 million historical shopping website data provided by Alibaba Big Data Competition, achieving near the competition’s top score, ranked as top 5% in class.
•	Proposed novel features to quantify the connections between users, shops and items information in the raw data.
•	Handled the imbalanced dataset (two labels are 50:1 distributed) by weighting, resampling, generating synthetic samples.
•	Compared prediction results of Regularized Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, modified Random Forest, Light GBM.

projects.name: Analysis of Supply-demand Relationship and Pricing Strategies in Two-sided Market
projects.description:
•	Employed state-dependent Jackson queueing network; combined the network effects of the two-sided markets to characterize the stochastic dynamics of passengers and drivers in the platform.
•	Proposed problem-specific numerical algorithm to solve the network equilibrium; formulated mathematical programming to maximize platform profit.
•	Discussed both static and dynamic pricing strategy by numerical experiment on randomly generated data.
